title: TrustPolicyRegistry Smart Contract: Policy Definitions and Scope Binding
description: Implement the TrustPolicyRegistry contract. A trust policy binds a service (identified by serviceId) to a set of allowed issuer DIDs and required VC types. Producer Sentinels query this registry to enforce which issuers/credential types are accepted for a given service. Policies have version numbers for auditability.
objectives: On-chain binding of service identifiers to trust requirements
Support multi-issuer policies (a service may accept credentials from multiple issuers)
Version history retained for audit — old policies not deleted, only superseded
Integrate with IssuerRegistry: validate that registered issuers exist before binding

steps: Step 1 - Contract Inheritance: AccessControl, Pausable (OpenZeppelin v5). Role: POLICY_ADMIN_ROLE = keccak256('POLICY_ADMIN_ROLE').

Step 2 - TrustPolicy Struct: struct TrustPolicy { string serviceId; string[] allowedIssuerDids; string[] requiredCredentialTypes; uint256 version; uint256 createdAt; uint256 updatedAt; bool active; string description; }

Step 3 - Storage: mapping(bytes32 => TrustPolicy) private _policies; key = keccak256(serviceId). mapping(bytes32 => TrustPolicy[]) private _policyHistory; stores all previous versions. bytes32[] private _policyKeys for enumeration.

Step 4 - Events: event PolicyCreated(bytes32 indexed serviceIdHash, string serviceId, uint256 version, address indexed createdBy); event PolicyUpdated(bytes32 indexed serviceIdHash, string serviceId, uint256 newVersion, address indexed updatedBy); event PolicyDeactivated(bytes32 indexed serviceIdHash, string serviceId, address indexed deactivatedBy);

Step 5 - createPolicy(string calldata serviceId, string[] calldata allowedIssuerDids, string[] calldata requiredCredentialTypes, string calldata description): onlyRole(POLICY_ADMIN_ROLE). Validate: serviceId > 0, allowedIssuerDids.length >= 1. Require: !_policies[hash].active (no existing active policy for this service). Set version=1. Emit PolicyCreated.

Step 6 - updatePolicy(string calldata serviceId, string[] calldata allowedIssuerDids, string[] calldata requiredCredentialTypes, string calldata description): onlyRole(POLICY_ADMIN_ROLE). Copy current policy to _policyHistory. Increment version. Update fields. Emit PolicyUpdated.
Step 7 - deactivatePolicy(string calldata serviceId): onlyRole(POLICY_ADMIN_ROLE). Sets active=false. Emit PolicyDeactivated.

Step 8 - Read Functions: getPolicy(string calldata serviceId) returns (TrustPolicy memory). isPolicyActive(string calldata serviceId) returns (bool). getPolicyHistory(string calldata serviceId) returns (TrustPolicy[] memory) — returns all historical versions. getPolicyCount() returns (uint256). isIssuerAllowedForService(string calldata serviceId, string calldata issuerDid) returns (bool) — convenience function.
Step 9 - ITrustPolicyRegistry Interface: in contracts/interfaces/ITrustPolicyRegistry.sol. Export all external views and mutating functions.

Step 10 - IssuerRegistry Reference: Store IssuerRegistry address in constructor. In createPolicy and updatePolicy, call IIssuerRegistry(issuerRegistry).isIssuerActive(did) for each allowedIssuerDid — if any issuer is not active, revert with UnknownIssuer(did). This prevents binding policies to unregistered issuers.
security_constraints: Cannot create a policy with zero allowed issuers — must have at least one
isIssuerAllowedForService iterates allowedIssuerDids array — cap array at 100 entries to prevent gas limit issues
Policy history is retained permanently — no deletion mechanism
IssuerRegistry address is immutable after construction — set in constructor, no setter

acceptance_criteria: createPolicy stores policy and emits PolicyCreated
updatePolicy saves old version to history and emits PolicyUpdated
createPolicy reverts if an allowedIssuerDid is not active in IssuerRegistry
isIssuerAllowedForService returns correct boolean for each case
